novel_features.name: Unanswered sent mail
novel_features.description: List emails you sent more than N days ago that the recipient never replied to — close the loops you forgot about.
novel_features.rationale: Joins local sent-mail against later received-mail by conversation_id and recipient address; Microsoft Graph exposes no such endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Sales, PM, and recruiting workflows live and die on follow-up timing. Reach for this when an agent needs to surface stalled threads.

novel_features.name: Sender volume rollup
novel_features.description: Group your inbox by sender over a window: count, unread, last-received, dominant folder — see who's drowning your inbox.
novel_features.rationale: SQL GROUP BY over the synced local store; Outlook UI groups by date, not by sender volume.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use as the input to bulk-archive or to drive inbox-rule decisions. Agents pruning a flooded inbox start here.

novel_features.name: What arrived since
novel_features.description: Show what landed since a timestamp, grouped by focused/other, sender, and folder. Perfect for 'what did I miss while I was heads-down'.
novel_features.rationale: received_at filter against the local delta-synced store. Graph's per-call filters can't reach across folders without N round-trips.
novel_features.why_it_matters: The first command an agent should run when joining a session to catch up. Cheap, structured, agent-shaped.

novel_features.name: Open flagged todos
novel_features.description: List flagged messages with open due dates, days-overdue, age. The inbox-zero work list Outlook never aggregates.
novel_features.rationale: Filter on flag_status with due-date diff over the local store; Graph returns flag fields but no aggregate todo view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: An agent's todo list. Pair with reply/forward to actually close the items.

novel_features.name: Stale unread report
novel_features.description: Unread messages older than N days, grouped by folder. Surfaces the unread debt hiding in subfolders.
novel_features.rationale: is_read filter + age window grouped by parent_folder_id over local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Different from `senders` (volume) — answers 'where is unread piling up' so an agent can route folders to bulk-mark-read.

novel_features.name: Waiting-on-me conversations
novel_features.description: Conversations where the last message is not from you and is unread or unanswered for N days. The 'someone's waiting on you' work list.
novel_features.rationale: Window function partitioned by conversation_id over local messages selects the last message and filters from_email != me AND unread.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Symmetric to followup. Pair both at start-of-day to see your half and their half of every open thread.

novel_features.name: Top conversations
novel_features.description: Conversations ranked by message count, participants, and unread tail. Find the threads burning your attention budget.
novel_features.rationale: GROUP BY conversation_id over local store with count + unread-tail aggregates; no Graph endpoint aggregates conversations.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Helps an agent decide which thread to summarize or mute next.

novel_features.name: Quiet senders
novel_features.description: Senders you used to hear from but who've gone silent for N days. Useful for relationship lapse detection.
novel_features.rationale: Self-join over local messages: ≥K messages in a baseline window, 0 in trailing window.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Sales/PM use case: surface customers or vendors who used to engage and don't anymore.

novel_features.name: Daily digest
novel_features.description: One-shot daily summary: received/sent/unread/flagged counts, top senders, top conversations, focused/other ratio.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregations over the local store for a single calendar date; rolled-up structure agents can deliver as a morning brief.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run this from a cron or skill loop to produce a Slack-shaped daily summary without making 10+ Graph calls.

novel_features.name: Stale attachments
novel_features.description: Attachments older than N days and over a size threshold, sortable by size. The mailbox-quota rescue plan.
novel_features.rationale: Joins attachment metadata (captured during sync) with message.received_at; Outlook web UI buries sort-by-size per folder.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the mailbox is near quota, agents need the largest oldest attachments first; this command is that list.

novel_features.name: Likely duplicates
novel_features.description: Find probable duplicate threads or messages by conversation_id, internet_message_id, or normalized (subject, from, to).
novel_features.rationale: GROUP BY with HAVING COUNT(*)>1 over the local store; no Graph endpoint clusters duplicates.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Surfaces newsletter duplicates and cross-folder copies before bulk-archive runs.

novel_features.name: Plan-then-execute bulk archive
novel_features.description: Read a sender list or query, print a move plan, optionally execute it. Safe by default, no surprises.
novel_features.rationale: Resolves matching message IDs locally, prints the move plan; --execute calls POST /me/messages/{id}/move per id. Side-effect command: print by default, short-circuits under PRINTING_PRESS_VERIFY=1.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pair with `senders` or `quiet` for end-to-end inbox triage; agents can audit the plan before opting in to --execute.

narrative.headline: Drive your personal Microsoft 365 inbox from agents — read, send, sync, and run offline triage analytics that Outlook's own UI never exposes.
narrative.value_prop: Personal MSA support via OAuth 2.0 device-code against /common. A local SQLite store synced through messages/delta unlocks `followup`, `senders`, `since`, `waiting`, `digest`, `stale-unread`, and `bulk-archive` — workflows no other Outlook CLI exposes because they require persisted state. Companion to outlook-calendar with the same auth playbook.
narrative.auth_narrative: OAuth 2.0 device-code flow against `https://login.microsoftonline.com/common`. Personal MSAs (Outlook.com, Hotmail, Live) work alongside work/school accounts. Default client id is the Microsoft Graph PowerShell app, so no Azure tenant is needed. BYO Azure AD app via `--client-id`. Refresh tokens persisted at `~/.config/outlook-email-pp-cli/config.toml`; run `auth refresh` or let commands auto-rotate.

quickstart.comment: One-time: sign in with a personal MSA via device-code flow.
Pull the full mailbox into the local SQLite store. Subsequent syncs are delta.
Catch up on what arrived since last check, grouped by focused/other and sender.
Surface emails you sent that nobody replied to.
See who's drowning your inbox — input to bulk-archive.

troubleshoots.fix: Run `outlook-email-pp-cli auth refresh`. If still failing, `auth status` shows scope and expiry.
Refresh token expired (>90 days). Run `auth login --device-code` again.

troubleshoots.symptom: Mailbox not enabled for personal MSA
troubleshoots.fix: Open Outlook.com once with the account to provision the Exchange mailbox; then retry sync.
Run `sync --folder Inbox --reset` to drop the delta token and re-pull the folder.

troubleshoots.symptom: Search returns nothing but inbox has matches
troubleshoots.fix: Local search reads the SQLite FTS5 index — run `sync` first. Use `--server` to force `$search` against Graph.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI when an agent needs to triage a personal Microsoft 365 inbox at scale, surface follow-ups, or compose mail without a Copilot license. It is the right choice over generic HTTP because the offline store unlocks cross-folder/cross-time queries (followup, senders, waiting, digest) that require persisted state.

recipes.title: Morning catchup
recipes.explanation: Delta-sync the mailbox, then surface only what landed overnight, grouped for an agent to summarize.

recipes.title: Follow-up sweep
recipes.explanation: Emails you sent more than a week ago with no reply — the loops you owe yourself to close.

recipes.title: Top noisy senders
recipes.explanation: Highest-volume senders over the last 30 days. Feed the output to `bulk-archive` to plan the prune.

recipes.title: Reply with selected fields only
recipes.explanation: Narrow a deeply-nested message payload to the four fields an agent actually needs before composing a reply.

recipes.title: Plan an inbox cleanup
recipes.explanation: Reads a sender list from senders.txt (one address per line), resolves matching messages in the local store, and prints the move plan. Pass --execute to actually call POST /me/messages/{id}/move per id.

trigger_phrases: follow up on email I sent
